summary: An almost‑perfect early‑80s audience tape that sounds like a soundboard, capturing a high‑energy set highlighted by scorching Althea, blazing Bertha and sublime Sugaree.
review: Glassberg’s spring ’81 audience recording (MOTB) is praised as the gold standard for live Dead tapes – crystal clear, balanced and virtually free of hiss, giving it near‑soundboard fidelity. The performance itself is a quintessential Brent‑era show: Jerry tears through Althea, Bertha and a jaw‑dropping Other One → Stella Blue transition, while the band stays tight from Peggy‑O to Sailor>Saint. Reviewers overwhelmingly describe the night as electrifying and unforgettable.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 70 reviews, almost all five‑star, consistently rave about both the exceptional audience recording quality and the high‑octane musical performance; only a handful of low‑rating outliers exist.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Glassberg + MOTB tape described as crystal‑clear, balanced and “soundboard quality”; minimal hiss, superb crowd ambience, no noticeable drop‑outs or muffiness.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Tight early‑80s Brent era set with relentless energy, seamless transitions (e.g., The Other One into Stella Blue) and a powerful second‑set climax that keeps the crowd dancing from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for blistering speed, soaring tone on Althea, Bertha and Sugaree, plus an unexpected Bach‑style quote during The Other One → Stella Blue.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s organ work highlighted as a driving force; several reviewers note his “favoring the organ” and solid support throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drummer (Bill) receives kudos for tight, propulsive playing that fuels The Other One and drives the high‑octane feel.
